name: Goa, India
description: India's beach capital - palm-lined sands from buzzing Calangute and Anjuna in the north to chilled Palolem in the south. Portuguese colonial churches, fresh seafood shacks, and India's most relaxed nightlife.

name: Andaman Islands, India
description: Pristine white-sand beaches and turquoise waters. Havelock (Swaraj Dweep) and Neil Island are top picks. Radhanagar Beach is consistently rated Asia's best beach. Excellent snorkelling, scuba diving, and very few crowds.

name: Varkala, India
description: Dramatic red laterite cliffs hanging over Papanasam Beach. A backpacker favourite for yoga, ayurveda treatments, surfing, and sunset views from cliff-side cafes.

name: Gokarna, India
description: A laid-back temple town with a string of secluded beaches reachable on a coastal trek - Kudle, Om, Half Moon, Paradise. Bohemian atmosphere with a strong spiritual undertone.

name: Pondicherry, India
description: Former French colony with pastel-yellow streets, baguettes, French street names, and beachfront promenades. Auroville commune nearby. Mix of European charm and Tamil culture.

name: Manali, India
description: Himalayan hill station in the Kullu Valley. Snow in winter, paragliding and rafting in summer, gateway to Ladakh and Spiti. Old Manali has cafes and backpacker vibes.

name: Shimla, India
description: Former summer capital of British India, perched at 2,200m. The Mall Road, Christ Church and toy train (UNESCO) are highlights. Pine forests, colonial mansions, mountain views.

name: Munnar, India
description: Rolling carpets of emerald tea plantations across Kerala's Western Ghats. Cool climate, waterfalls, spice gardens, Eravikulam National Park (Nilgiri tahr). Perfect for honeymooners.

name: Ooty, India
description: Queen of Hill Stations in the Nilgiris. Cool, eucalyptus-scented air, botanical gardens, the iconic Nilgiri Mountain Railway (UNESCO). Tea estates and chocolate factories.

name: Darjeeling, India
description: World-famous tea region with views of Kanchenjunga, the world's third-highest peak. Sunrise from Tiger Hill, the toy train, and Tibetan/Nepali culture create a unique vibe.

name: Coorg, India
description: India's coffee country in the Karnataka hills, known as the 'Scotland of India'. Coffee plantations, waterfalls, ancient Kodava culture, hiking trails, and homestays.

name: Agra, India
description: Home to the Taj Mahal, one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. Add Agra Fort and Fatehpur Sikri (both UNESCO) for a full Mughal heritage day.

name: Jaipur, India
description: The Pink City - Rajasthan's capital and the gateway to the Golden Triangle. Royal palaces, hilltop forts, vibrant bazaars, and colourful turbans everywhere.

name: Udaipur, India
description: The City of Lakes, often called the 'Venice of the East'. Whitewashed havelis around Lake Pichola, the floating Taj Lake Palace, and the colossal City Palace.

name: Jodhpur, India
description: The Blue City - clusters of indigo-blue houses cascading below the towering Mehrangarh Fort. Gateway to the Thar Desert and Jaisalmer.

name: Hampi, India
description: UNESCO ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire scattered across a surreal boulder-strewn landscape. Sunset from Matanga Hill is otherworldly. Backpacker favourite.

name: Khajuraho, India
description: UNESCO temples (10th-11th c.) famous for intricate sculptures depicting daily life and the kama tradition. Sound-and-light show in the evenings.

name: Varanasi, India
description: One of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities. Sacred ghats along the Ganges, dawn boat rides, evening Ganga Aarti, ancient temples, and an intense spiritual atmosphere.

name: Rishikesh, India
description: Yoga capital of the world on the banks of the Ganges. White-water rafting, bungee jumping, ashrams, riverside cafes, and the Beatles' famous ashram nearby.

name: Leh-Ladakh, India
description: High-altitude desert at 3,500m+. Lunar landscapes, Tibetan Buddhist monasteries, Pangong Lake, Nubra Valley dunes, and the world's highest motorable passes. Bike trip bucket-list.

name: Spiti Valley, India
description: Cold desert valley dubbed 'Little Tibet'. Ancient monasteries (Key, Tabo, Dhankar), the world's highest village (Komic), and dramatic moonscape passes. Less touristy than Ladakh.

name: Meghalaya, India
description: The 'abode of clouds' - Cherrapunji and Mawsynram are among Earth's wettest places. Living root bridges, crystal-clear Dawki river, limestone caves, and sweeping cliff views.

name: Tawang, India
description: High-altitude border town home to India's largest monastery (and the world's second-largest Tibetan Buddhist one). Spectacular drive over Sela Pass through frozen lakes and snow walls.

name: Sikkim, India
description: Pristine Himalayan state with Kanchenjunga views from Pelling, the rhododendron-lined Yumthang Valley, sacred Tsomgo Lake, and Buddhist monasteries. India's first 100% organic state.

name: Lakshadweep, India
description: Coral atolls in the Arabian Sea - only Indians can visit (special permit). Bangaram and Agatti rival the Maldives for clear lagoons. Excellent scuba and snorkelling, almost no crowds.

name: Alleppey, India
description: Kerala's iconic backwaters - overnight on a kettuvallam (rice-barge houseboat) gliding through palm-lined canals, paddy fields, and quiet villages. Snake-boat races in monsoon.

name: Mysore, India
description: City of Palaces. Mysore Palace lit up nightly with 100,000 bulbs is unforgettable. Birthplace of Ashtanga yoga, the centre of Indian sandalwood, and host to a famous Dussehra festival.

name: Mumbai, India
description: India's financial and entertainment capital. Victorian-Gothic colonial architecture, Marine Drive sunsets, Bollywood, and the world's most chaotic street food. Gateway to Elephanta Caves.

name: Delhi, India
description: India's capital - three UNESCO sites (Red Fort, Humayun's Tomb, Qutub Minar), the chaos of Old Delhi, the order of Lutyens' Delhi, and unbeatable street food in Chandni Chowk.

name: Kasol & Parvati Valley, India
description: Backpacker hangout known as 'Mini Israel' for its hippie cafes, riverside camping, and treks to Kheerganga's hot springs. Pine forests and the rushing Parvati river.

name: Bali, Indonesia
description: Indonesia's Island of the Gods. Ubud's rice terraces, Uluwatu's clifftop temples and surf, Seminyak's beach clubs, Canggu's digital-nomad scene, and Mt Batur sunrise treks.

name: Ubud, Bali, Indonesia
description: Bali's spiritual and cultural heartland. Lush jungle, terraced rice paddies, yoga retreats, traditional dance, and warung food. Sacred Monkey Forest in the centre of town.

name: Yogyakarta, Indonesia
description: Java's cultural capital. Borobudur (world's largest Buddhist temple) and Prambanan (Hindu) - both UNESCO. Royal Sultan's palace, batik workshops, and volcano-jeep tours of Mt Merapi.

name: Bangkok, Thailand
description: Frenetic, fragrant, fascinating. Glittering temples (Wat Pho, Wat Arun, Grand Palace), tuk-tuks, floating markets, riverside dining, and a legendary street-food scene.

name: Chiang Mai, Thailand
description: Walled old city dotted with 300+ Buddhist temples. Hill-tribe villages, ethical elephant sanctuaries, jungle treks, Sunday Walking Street market, and a thriving digital-nomad scene.

name: Phuket, Thailand
description: Thailand's largest island. Patong's wild nightlife, Karon's quieter sands, hopping to Phi Phi or James Bond Island, kid-friendly resorts, and old town's Sino-Portuguese architecture.

name: Krabi, Thailand
description: Limestone karst cliffs jutting from emerald water. Railay Beach for rock climbing, Ao Nang as base, and four-island tours to dazzling lagoons. Quieter and more scenic than Phuket.

name: Pai, Thailand
description: Tiny mountain town reached by 762 hairpin bends north of Chiang Mai. Hot springs, waterfalls, scooter loops, and a famous laid-back hippie atmosphere.

name: Singapore
description: Compact city-state mixing futuristic skyline (Marina Bay Sands, Gardens by the Bay) with hawker-centre street food, ethnic enclaves (Little India, Chinatown, Kampong Glam), and family-friendly Sentosa.

name: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
description: Multicultural capital where mosques, Hindu temples, and skyscrapers stand side by side. Petronas Towers, Batu Caves, and a serious street-food game.

name: Penang, Malaysia
description: George Town is a UNESCO heritage town famed for street art, colonial shophouses, and Asia's best food scene. Beaches and tropical hills on the rest of the island.

name: Langkawi, Malaysia
description: Archipelago of 99 islands with white sands, the SkyCab cable car (one of the world's steepest), mangrove tours, and duty-free everything.

name: Ha Long Bay, Vietnam
description: UNESCO seascape of 1,600+ limestone karst islands rising from emerald water. Overnight cruise on a junk boat with kayaking through hidden lagoons and caves.

name: Hoi An, Vietnam
description: UNESCO ancient trading port lit by silk lanterns each evening. Famous for tailors who make custom clothes in 24 hours, riverside cafes, and An Bang Beach nearby.

name: Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
description: Vietnam's frenetic southern hub. War Remnants Museum, Cu Chi tunnels, French colonial relics, and a banh mi every block. Backpacker base for the Mekong Delta.

name: Siem Reap, Cambodia
description: Gateway to Angkor - the largest religious monument on Earth. Sunrise over Angkor Wat is bucket-list. Temples deep in the jungle (Ta Prohm), pub street, and Cambodian markets.

name: Ella, Sri Lanka
description: Misty hills, tea plantations, and the world-famous train ride from Kandy through nine-arch bridges. Hike Little Adam's Peak and Ella Rock for sunrise views.

name: Sigiriya, Sri Lanka
description: 5th-century rock fortress rising 200m from the jungle floor. UNESCO Lion Rock with ancient frescoes and water gardens. Climb at sunrise for cool weather and crowd-free views.

name: Galle, Sri Lanka
description: Dutch-walled fortress town on the southern coast. Cobbled streets inside the UNESCO fort, ramparts watching the Indian Ocean, plus surf beaches at Unawatuna and Mirissa nearby.

name: Pokhara, Nepal
description: Nepal's adventure capital on Phewa Lake with the Annapurna range as backdrop. Paragliding, the famous Annapurna Base Camp / Poon Hill treks, and lakeside cafes.

name: Kathmandu, Nepal
description: Seven UNESCO sites in one valley - Boudhanath stupa, Pashupatinath, Bhaktapur and Patan Durbar Squares. Thamel's chaotic backpacker streets and trek-prep central.

name: Maldives
description: 1,200 coral atolls in turquoise water - overwater bungalows, world-class scuba and snorkelling on house reefs, manta rays and whale sharks. Bucket-list honeymoon destination.

name: Bhutan
description: The 'Last Shangri-La' that measures success in Gross National Happiness. Tiger's Nest monastery clinging to a cliff, Punakha's fortress, Paro's valley, and a strict tourism cap that keeps it pristine.

name: Tokyo, Japan
description: Sprawling, futuristic capital where ancient temples sit beside neon skyscrapers. Sushi at Toyosu, robots in Shinjuku, cherry blossoms in spring, and the world's most efficient trains.

name: Kyoto, Japan
description: Japan's old imperial capital - 2,000 temples and shrines, geisha-spotted Gion, the orange torii of Fushimi Inari, and the bamboo forest of Arashiyama. Spring sakura is iconic.

name: Seoul, South Korea
description: K-pop, kimchi, and 600-year-old palaces side by side with futuristic neighbourhoods like Gangnam. Korean BBQ, late-night street food at Myeongdong, and DMZ tours.

name: Hong Kong
description: Vertical city of skyscrapers, dim-sum palaces, hidden hiking trails, and night markets. Take the Star Ferry across the harbour and the Peak Tram up Victoria Peak for the iconic skyline.

name: Paris, France
description: The City of Light - Eiffel Tower, the Louvre, sidewalk cafes, croissants, the Seine. World-class museums, charming Montmartre, and sublime food at every level.

name: Rome, Italy
description: Open-air museum. The Colosseum, Roman Forum, Pantheon, and Vatican City all in walking distance. Trastevere for trattorias, Trevi for coins, gelato all day.

name: Barcelona, Spain
description: Gaudi's surreal cityscape - Sagrada Familia, Park Guell, Casa Batllo. Plus Gothic Quarter alleys, Boqueria food market, La Barceloneta beach, and tapas crawls until 3am.

name: Santorini, Greece
description: Volcanic caldera ringed by white-and-blue villages cascading down the cliff. Oia's iconic sunset, black-sand beaches at Perissa, and ancient Akrotiri ruins.

name: Amsterdam, Netherlands
description: Canal-ringed centre, gabled merchant houses, world-class art (Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh, Anne Frank House), and a bicycle culture unlike anywhere else.

name: Prague, Czech Republic
description: Fairytale skyline of Gothic spires and the largest ancient castle in the world. Charles Bridge at dawn, the astronomical clock, beer cheaper than water, and bohemian Zizkov.

name: Switzerland (Interlaken)
description: Alpine adventure base between Lake Thun and Lake Brienz. Jungfrau region for snow trains and skiing, paragliding off Beatenberg, and cogwheel rails up to Schilthorn.

name: London, UK
description: Multicultural capital with royal pomp (Buckingham Palace, Tower of London), free world-class museums (British, Tate, V&A), West End theatre, and pub culture in every borough.

name: New York City, USA
description: The ultimate city - Times Square, Broadway, Central Park, Statue of Liberty, world-class museums, and the most diverse food scene on the planet. Never sleeps.

name: Cusco / Machu Picchu, Peru
description: Former Inca capital at 3,400m and gateway to Machu Picchu, the famous lost city. Sacred Valley, Rainbow Mountain, and the Inca Trail trek all radiate out.

name: Banff, Canada
description: Postcard Rockies - turquoise Lake Louise and Moraine Lake, hot springs, hiking, and the Icefields Parkway scenic drive. Skiing in winter, paddling in summer.

name: Dubai, UAE
description: Ultra-modern Gulf city of record-breaking skyscrapers (Burj Khalifa), giant malls, indoor ski slopes, desert safaris, and palm-shaped islands. Layover stop turned bucket-list.

name: Istanbul, Turkey
description: Where Europe and Asia meet across the Bosphorus. Hagia Sophia and Blue Mosque rise from Sultanahmet, Grand Bazaar's 4,000 shops sprawl below, and rooftop restaurants own the sunset.

name: Marrakech, Morocco
description: Sensory overload medina - winding souks, riads with hidden courtyards, Jemaa el-Fnaa square at dusk with snake-charmers and food stalls. Gateway to the Sahara and Atlas Mountains.

name: Cape Town, South Africa
description: Table Mountain looming over the city, Cape Point's wild peninsula, Boulders Beach penguins, Stellenbosch wine farms, and the dark heritage of Robben Island.

name: Sydney, Australia
description: Iconic harbour city - Opera House, Harbour Bridge climb, surf at Bondi, ferry to Manly, and the Blue Mountains an hour west. Coastal walks rival the world.

name: Queenstown, New Zealand
description: Adventure capital of the world on Lake Wakatipu. Bungee jumping (where it was invented), jet boats, sky-diving, glacier hikes, and the Lord-of-the-Rings-worthy Milford Sound nearby.
